_comment: DRAFT — Knee and ACL recovery exercises. Not yet clinically reviewed. Do not integrate into runtime code.

exercises.name: Short Arc Quad
exercises.description: Place a rolled towel under the knee to support it at approximately 40°. From this position, straighten the knee to full extension and lower slowly. Targets the terminal range of quadriceps contraction.
exercises.cue: Focus on the last 30° of knee straightening — that is where the VMO works hardest.
exercises.painNote: Mild anterior knee ache at end range is common. Stop for sharp or joint-line pain.
exercises.imageAlt: Person lying on back with rolled towel under knee, extending the lower leg to straight
exercises.progressionNotes: Progress to terminal knee extension with resistance band.

exercises.name: Wall Squat (Isometric)
exercises.description: Stand with your back flat against a wall, feet shoulder-width apart and about 30 cm from the wall. Slide down until the knees are at 45–60° and hold. A low-load way to build quad endurance with joint support from the wall.
exercises.cue: Keep your back flat against the wall. Do not let your hips sag below knee height.
exercises.painNote: Avoid if anterior knee pain is over 3/10. Start with 30° knee bend if needed.
exercises.imageAlt: Person with back against wall, knees bent to approximately 60 degrees
exercises.progressionNotes: Increase hold duration first, then increase knee flexion depth.

exercises.name: Step-Down (Eccentric)
exercises.description: Stand on a step on the operated leg. Slowly lower the opposite foot toward the floor by bending the standing knee, then return to start without touching the floor. Eccentric quad loading — essential for return-to-sport.
exercises.cue: Keep your knee tracking over your second toe. Do not let it drift inward as you lower.
exercises.painNote: Anterior knee pain over 4/10 — reduce step height. Never load into sharp pain.
exercises.imageAlt: Person standing on one leg on a step, slowly bending the knee to lower the free leg
exercises.progressionNotes: Progress to higher step (30 cm) then add a weighted vest.

exercises.name: Prone Hamstring Curl
exercises.description: Lying face down, bend the knee to bring the heel toward the buttocks and lower slowly. Can be performed with a resistance band around the ankle. Rebuilds hamstring strength — critical for ACL protection.
exercises.cue: Keep your hips pressed into the mat. Do not let the pelvis rock as you curl.
exercises.painNote: If you feel pulling behind the knee rather than in the hamstring, reduce the range of motion.
exercises.imageAlt: Person lying face down bending one knee to curl the heel toward the buttocks
contraindications: hamstring graft ACL reconstruction (early stages — confirm with surgeon)
exercises.progressionNotes: Add resistance band. Progress to Nordic curl in Phase 3.

exercises.name: Nordic Hamstring Curl
exercises.description: Kneel on a padded surface with feet anchored under a strap, bench, or partner. Slowly lower your torso toward the floor by allowing the knees to extend, catching yourself with your hands, then use the hamstrings to pull back up. One of the most effective hamstring strengthening exercises for injury prevention.
exercises.cue: Stay as long as you can on the way down — the eccentric phase is the whole point.
exercises.painNote: Significant hamstring DOMS (delayed onset muscle soreness) is expected in the first week. Not suitable for hamstring graft ACL until 6+ months post-op — check with your surgeon.
exercises.imageAlt: Person kneeling with feet anchored, lowering their body toward the floor in a controlled eccentric movement
exercises.progressionNotes: Increase reps once 3×6 full-range is achieved.

exercises.name: Single-Leg Squat
exercises.description: Stand on one leg and squat to approximately 60° of knee flexion, maintaining balance and alignment. The benchmark test for quadriceps strength symmetry and return-to-sport readiness.
exercises.tempo: 3 s down — 1 s pause — 2 s up
exercises.cue: Keep your knee directly over your second toe and your hips level — do not let the opposite hip drop.
exercises.painNote: If knee caves inward involuntarily, regress to assisted single-leg squat using a TRX or wall for balance.
exercises.imageAlt: Person performing a controlled single-leg squat on a gym floor
exercises.progressionNotes: Limb symmetry index target: 90%. Progress to plyometric loading once symmetry is confirmed.

exercises.name: Lateral Step-Up
exercises.description: Standing beside a step, place one foot on the step and drive up sideways through the hip and glute. Challenges the hip abductors and glute medius in a weight-bearing position — important for dynamic knee control.
exercises.cue: Drive the movement from the glute — push your hip out and up over the foot on the step.
exercises.painNote: Knee pain at the top of the movement — check hip alignment and reduce step height.
exercises.imageAlt: Person stepping laterally onto a step, driving through the hip and glute of the supporting leg
exercises.progressionNotes: Add a dumbbell or weighted vest once 3×10 is confident.

exercises.name: Bilateral Box Jump
exercises.description: Jump with both feet onto a low box, landing with soft knees in an athletic position. Introduces plyometric loading to prepare the knee for sport. Only appropriate after strength symmetry is confirmed (>90% limb symmetry index).
exercises.tempo: Explosive jump — stick the landing for 2 s
exercises.cue: Land quietly — soft knees, hips back. The sound of landing tells you everything about your technique.
exercises.painNote: Do not progress to this exercise without coach sign-off. Requires confirmed quadriceps symmetry >90%. Stop if any sharp knee pain on landing.
exercises.imageAlt: Person jumping onto a low plyometric box, landing with bent knees in athletic stance
contraindications: <12 months post ACL reconstruction without surgeon clearance
exercises.progressionNotes: Progress to single-leg box jump once bilateral confidence is established.
